message: And on the seventh day God ended his work which he had made; and he rested on the seventh day from all his work which he had made.
And God blessed the seventh day,
because that in it he had rested from all his work which God created and made.
The day he finished creating the world.
Even the weekend doesn't change one bit.
God doesn't change the color of the sky depending on the day of the week.
The world God created contains the same sights every single day.
It invariably arrives back at the same scenery over and over, like a mechanical clock.
It rotates through the same sequence.
A person standing in front of a clock...
Doesn't think a new moment begins...
With every tick, tock of the clock's hands.
When the clock hand passes twelve o'clock, the summit...
No one thinks that a new time has begun.
An end is the beginning of another commonplace scene.
The beginning of a commonplace world.
And beyond that, the scenery one just saw comes into existence once more.
An infinity of the same script.
There is nothing novel in this world.
All that is in this world can be clearly described.
There is nothing beyond this world.
And the next world we're headed to... It is all part of a world that can be described in the same terms.
I... had no reason to be here.
I just ran, and then I was standing here.
At some point, my world had started to go askew.
At some point, my world had started to mingle with a scene I'd never seen before.

name: Yuki
message: "What's that? I don't know what you mean."

name: Yuki
message: "What's going on? When did all this happen?"

name: Yuki
message: "Wakatsuki Kagami really existed, right? Wakatsuki Tsukasa really existed, right?"

name: Yuki
message: "I mean, I... I have all these memories with them. All these memories from so long ago."

name: Yuki
message: "Why did Kagami's body turn into a doll? Why did Tsukasa turn into that scary thing?"

name: Yuki
message: "When did they get replaced like that?"

name: Ayana
message: "That's why you came to see it, right?"

name: Yuki
message: "What's the End Sky? Does that have something to do with what's happening?!"

name: Ayana
message: "The End Sky is just what Mamiya Takuji-kun called it."

name: Yuki
message: "What do you mean? Why doesn't it have a name?"

name: Ayana
message: "Just like how there's no name for the sky at the end of the week, that sky doesn't have a name either."

name: Yuki
message: "I wish it was something as nice as a mystery. This is impossible and illogical."

name: Ayana
message: "From the time they're born, people replace the cells in their bodies several times over."

name: Ayana
message: "Just like your body whose cells have been replaced time and time again, you have remained you despite the fact that you have constantly changed since the moment of your birth."

name: Ayana
message: "Nothing changes. Minakami Yuki is Minakami Yuki. There's nothing impossible or illogical about that."

name: Ayana
message: "Forty-five quadrillion, four hundred and twenty-five trillion, six hundred and ninety-eight billion, four hundred and fifty-two million, three hundred and fifty-five thousand, four hundred and eighty-one."

name: Ayana
message: "That is probably the first time I have said that number in this world."

name: Ayana
message: "It is an unknown figure that no one has heard or seen."

name: Ayana
message: "No one has heard this number, but no one would be shocked to hear it."

name: Ayana
message: "Even if no one has given it shape, anyone can understand it. Everyone already knows it."

name: Ayana
message: "Even if such a scene has never appeared in this world, such a scene should not frighten you."

name: Ayana
message: "It's a very commonplace sort of world."

name: Yuki
message: "How is that... How is this world commonplace? This..."

name: Ayana
message: "Like I said, you're going to go see what's beyond this commonplace world, right?"

name: Ayana
message: "And there's something you want to ask Mamiya-kun, isn't there?"
I couldn't even walk, since it had been less than ten seconds since we reached the bottom.
So she knew all about it.
There was no way they could gather in such a tiny place.
Ayana-san had already lifted up the cover inside and left.

name: Yuki
message: "I was thinking that tons of people could probably hide out in here."
It was like she already knew the way.
She walked over to the place where Kagami had been laying.
Where the floor was covered in dark red blood.
There was just cotton scattered everywhere.
Kagami's mutilated body was just... a doll.
What happened to the real Kagami?
Ayana-san stopped and stood in one spot.
A huge pillar stood in front of her. It seemed as if it was supporting the world itself.
The huge pillar was covered in scribbles, though they were carefully drawn, like religious idols.
From far away, they blended in with the rest of the pillar.

name: Yuki
message: "The whole thing is covered in scribbles?!"

name: Ayana
message: "That's right. This is the shape of Mamiya-kun's Nyarlathotep."

name: Yuki
message: "What? Is that Nyarl-whatever a living thing or something?"

name: Ayana
message: "It's time to go see the End Sky."
I'd thought it was pure darkness, but I caught a few vague glimpses of steel within it.
It looked like a spider web reaching down into the depths of hell.
I was caught up in the illusion that I was wandering through endless darkness.
I could hear the wind in the distance.
At the top of the ladder, I could hear the wind.
The far off sound of the wind slowly came closer.
The rooftop that students weren't allowed on.
It was the roof of building C.
I looked over to where she'd pointed.
What are they doing over there?
The spot Mrs. Senagawa fell from.
Why are those two standing there?

name: Takuji
message: "Oh, look. If it isn't Minakami-san. Long time, no see."

name: Takuji
message: "So you were still there. I just saved everyone."

name: Takuji
message: "People wish to become one with something greater, but that's no different from death. That's why they fear becoming one with something greater, just as they fear death."

name: Yuki
message: "I don't want to hear this drivel coming out of your crazy head."

name: Takuji
message: "Oh. Then is there something you do want to hear?"

name: Yuki
message: "Something I want to hear? Yeah, I've got plenty of questions for you."

name: Yuki
message: "You set this all up, didn't you?"

name: Yuki
message: "That's right. This whole thing is a farce you devised."

name: Takuji
message: "Excuse me, are you talking about my miracles?"

name: Takuji
message: "Oh. That's interesting. So you're saying there's a trick to my miracles?"

name: Yuki
message: "Right. There's no curse and no miracles. And, of course, there's no prophecy that the world's going to end. There's just a bunch of crude tricks."

name: Takuji
message: "Hahahahaha. Crude tricks? So that's what you think. Have you figured them all out?"

name: Takuji
message: "Heheheheh. That's wonderful. I suppose I should've expected that much of you, Minakami-san. Well then, let's hear it. Let's hear about all my tricks."

name: Takuji
message: "From the beginning. How did it start? Can you explain that?"

name: Yuki
message: "The beginning? Well then, the start of this uproar would be Takashima-san's curse mail, I suppose?"

name: Takuji
message: "Oh, the curse mail. I'd love to hear about that."

name: Yuki
message: "They call it Takashima mail. The cursed messages that got sent out to everyone. That was the simplest trick."

name: Yuki
message: "That's right. It was so obvious. The method was downright primitive."

name: Yuki
message: "You either picked up Takashima-san's phone at the site of her suicide, or you stole it."

name: Takuji
message: "Heheheheh. I see. That's an obvious trick, isn't it? So then, what's your proof?"

name: Yuki
message: "I found two sets of fingerprints on her phone. Takashima-san's own fingerprints, and yours."

name: Yuki
message: "I don't know if it was a prank or what, but someone left her phone in my room. It was you, right?"

name: Yuki
message: "You were probably trying to scare me, but you left way too much evidence behind. I also got fingerprints from your desk and Takashima-san's desk, and then I compared them."

name: Yuki
message: "It was a bingo. I confirmed that the fingerprints on the phone were Takashima Zakuro's and yours."

name: Yuki
message: "You used her phone and sent out those messages to as many people as you could."

name: Yuki
message: "I don't know how you got them, but the addresses you got were from the underground message board's registration list."

name: Takuji
message: "Heheheheheh. How I got them? So you still don't know that."

name: Yuki
message: "Unfortunately not, but it all lines up."

name: Yuki
message: "Well, it's just a hypothesis, so I have no real evidence, but if the police get involved, they'll figure it out right away."

name: Yuki
message: "The school's underground message board, the SAWAYAKA message board... You must be the administrator."

name: Yuki
message: "And if we assume that, the strange stuff happening in those threads makes sense too."

name: Takuji
message: "You mean, if I was the administrator, I would have all their registration information?"

name: Yuki
message: "You made the bullying she received the main point of the messages, and you used that information to make the people related to the bullying your primary targets."

name: Yuki
message: "As far as I know, those were Akasaka Megu, Kitami Satoko, and Mrs. Senagawa."

name: Takuji
message: "That is also correct, though I'd have to give you only 75%."

name: Takuji
message: "Right. The people who felt guilty about her bullying were Akasaka Megu, Kitami Satoko, the teacher who let it happen, Mrs. Senagawa, and then, one you forgot: Zakuro's friend, Tachibana Kimika."

name: Takuji
message: "Yes, now she is. She was the most useful follower I had."

name: Takuji
message: "But at first, she felt guilty about bullying Takashima Zakuro too."

name: Takuji
message: "But putting that aside, I'd like you to tell me. How did I corner Akasaka, Kitami, and Senagawa?"

name: Yuki
message: "They were posting all about it on the message board. They got messages from Takashima-san that no one else got."

name: Yuki
message: "Messages from the dead every hour. You used those to put them under extreme stress and stop them from thinking clearly."

name: Yuki
message: "The most elementary of all elementary social psychology techniques."

name: Yuki
message: "Mind control. It's what emergent religions do to gain followers, and it's what multi-level marketers do to find victims."

name: Yuki
message: "A lone prophet is just a nutcase, but if even one other person believes them, if they find even one follower, then it becomes a religion."

name: Yuki
message: "In order for you to become the savior, you needed to have at least one follower."

name: Yuki
message: "From looking at the message board, it seems like the first two to believe you were Akasaka Megu and Kitami Satoko. After you secured those two, your followers swelled."

name: Yuki
message: "The endless messages from the dead chased them into a corner. It made them ask for salvation."

name: Yuki
message: "If there was someone who could save them from that hell, that person would be able to call himself the savior."

name: Yuki
message: "And by doing it on the message board where other people could see, the effect was multiplied."

name: Yuki
message: "So in order to raise their stress as high as possible, you told them to go to the place they most feared, the place where Takashima Zakuro committed suicide."

name: Yuki
message: "Right, you told them to go to the site of her suicide in the middle of the night. What they found there was a lock of Takashima Zakuro's hair with part of her scalp still attached."

name: Yuki
message: "You probably got that at the same time you picked up her phone."

name: Yuki
message: "And you gave them the impression that they had dispelled the curse by having them burn the hair."

name: Yuki
message: "And you told them all about your beliefs while they were completely exhausted, both in mind and spirit."

name: Yuki
message: "You crushed their spirits with fear, and then filled them up with new information. It's the most elementary of elementary mind control techniques."

name: Yuki
message: "The same thing a predatory marketer does."

name: Yuki
message: "You made followers of the weak, and increased the anxiety of the rest."

name: Mamiya
message: "Heheheh. How wonderful. You really are amazing, Minakami-san."

name: Yuki
message: "You managed to get her cell phone, although I stole it from Ms. Kiyokawa."

name: Mamiya
message: "Yes, that's right. So then what did you learn from that?"

name: Yuki
message: "Almost all of the information had been deleted. The messages and everything."

name: Mamiya
message: "So then, what did you find out?"

name: Yuki
message: "Yes, with that brand of phone, I could retrieve all the deleted messages."

name: Yuki
message: "Messages came every hour. And there was nothing to them. They were just trying to scare her. And they suddenly started leading her in a specific direction on the 15th."

name: Yuki
message: "They started working to get her on the Building C rooftop."

name: Yuki
message: "And that made me remember how the 15th was the day when you made a strange prediction."

name: Yuki
message: "Something about another death proving you correct."

name: Takuji
message: "I did make a prediction like that."

name: Yuki
message: "The messages started mentioning the doll Takashima-san lost on Building C."

name: Yuki
message: "After all, if she couldn't find it, Takashima Zakuro would take her instead."

name: Yuki
message: "And the times she received the messages..."

name: Yuki
message: "Interestingly enough, all the messages were sent an hour apart."

name: Yuki
message: "But the timing of the very last message was completely different."

name: Yuki
message: "Because that message was right before she fell, at a time that didn't obey the schedule up until then."

name: Yuki
message: "You placed the doll she lost on the Building C rooftop in a very dangerous place."

name: Yuki
message: "Normally, someone would use a tool to get it, but at that time, Mrs. Senagawa wasn't thinking clearly."

name: Yuki
message: "She climbed over the fence, got down on the eaves, and then she stretched out her hand."

name: Yuki
message: "And just when she was least stable, her phone rang."

name: Takuji
message: "Heheheh. What a wonderful deduction, Minakami-san. You're almost totally correct."

name: Yuki
message: "Yeah, I've finally been able to put together the big picture. This whole farce was your doing."

name: Yuki
message: "It wasn't a supernatural phenomenon or God or a ghost or anything like that. Just a single person. You came up with the whole thing."

name: Yuki
message: "You broke into my room and left Takashima-san's phone. And Kagami and Tsukasa..."

name: Yuki
message: "Just what did you do to me?!"

name: Takuji
message: "Sorry to say, but I haven't done a single thing to you."

name: Yuki
message: "That's a lie! That can't be true! You did something to me!"

name: Takuji
message: "I didn't. I can't do anything to you."

name: Yuki
message: "Then what was that I saw?! What is all this?!"

name: Takuji
message: "It looks like you want to stay in this moment forever."
But I saw something. Did that kill everyone?
IT'S NOTHING. MAMIYA-KUN JUST JUMPED OFF.
No, I didn't do anything. Just...

MIYA-KUN JUST JUMPED OFF.""[is] a world nor
I heard the sound of my skull cracking.
But that wasn't from the outside world.
I think it came from within me.
Minakami-san, sometimes I think about these things.
Where is the limit of the world?
The very edge of the edge of the world.
Suppose I were able to stand in that place. Could I look at the scenery from that edge, just like anywhere else?
If I think it's normal to wonder about these things... that must make me weird.
I mean, that's the edge of the world.
It's the limit of the world.
If I could see that with my own two eyes, wouldn't the limit of the world become the same as my own limit?
Wouldn't the world, viewed from that spot... be the world that I see? Wouldn't that be my world?
The limit of the world would become the limit of my world.
The world is that which I see, touch, and feel.
What is the difference between me and the world?
The difference between me and the world.
However, other people might have felt this too.
Even you might think that the world is you.
And I think that's probably correct.
I couldn't say for sure, but if you stood at the edge of the world, you would probably see the shape of the world just like I do.
So you would be the same as the world too.
But that really doesn't make sense.
If the world is me, why can't I see the world that you see?
Even though you are in my world, I can't see the world you see.
I've never seen the world that you do.
They're like parallel universes that can never cross.
Even if there are hints that suggest the other exists, they can never touch.
I can't see the world you live in.
Have I really never seen your world?
If every person has been equally allotted one singular world...
How could those worlds possibly become one?
How can there be so many worlds?
There must be a way for the worlds to become one.
Sometimes, I think about these things.
I was able to love you.
